heading: Definitions

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Co-Chairs of the Task Force
children.label: 2 Co-Chairs of the Task Force
children.text: The term “Co-Chairs of the Task Force” means the Secretary of State, the Secretary of the Interior, and the Attorney General, as established pursuant to Executive Order 13648.

children.label: A
children.text: devolving management and governance to local communities to create positive conditions for resource use that takes into account current and future ecological requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: building the capacity of communities for conservation and natural resource management.

children.label: A
children.text: the government has actively engaged in, or knowingly profited from, the trafficking of protected species; or

children.label: B
children.text: the government facilitates such trafficking through conduct that may include a persistent failure to make serious and sustained efforts to prevent and prosecute such trafficking.

children.heading: Focus country
children.label: 5 Focus country
children.text: The term “focus country” refers to a foreign country determined by the Secretary of State to be a major source of wildlife trafficking products or their derivatives, a major transit point of wildlife trafficking products or their derivatives, or a major consumer of wildlife trafficking products.

children.heading: Defense article; defense service; significant military equipment; training
children.label: 6 Defense article; defense service; significant military equipment; training
children.text: The terms “defense article”, “defense service”, “significant military equipment”, and “training” have the meanings given such terms in .section 2794 of title 22

children.heading: Implementation Plan
children.label: 7 Implementation Plan
children.text: The term “Implementation Plan” means the Implementation Plan for the National Strategy for Combating Wildlife Trafficking released on , a modification of that plan, or a successor plan.February 11, 2015

children.heading: National Strategy
children.label: 8 National Strategy
children.text: The term “National Strategy” means the National Strategy for Combating Wildlife Trafficking published on , a modification of that strategy, or a successor strategy.February 11, 2014

children.heading: National wildlife services
children.label: 9 National wildlife services
children.text: The term “national wildlife services” refers to the ministries and government bodies designated to manage matters pertaining to wildlife management, including poaching or trafficking, in a focus country.

children.heading: Security force
children.label: 10 Security force
children.text: The term “security force” means a military, law enforcement, gendarmerie, park ranger, or any other security force with a responsibility for protecting wildlife and natural habitats.

children.heading: Task Force
children.label: 11 Task Force
children.text: The term “Task Force” means the Presidential Task Force on Wildlife Trafficking, as established by Executive Order 13648 (78 Fed. Reg. 40621) and modified by .section 7631 of this title

children.heading: Wildlife trafficking
children.label: 12 Wildlife trafficking
children.text: The term “wildlife trafficking” refers to the poaching or other illegal taking of protected or managed species and the illegal trade in wildlife and their related parts and products.
